---
title: "NY HP Rates — LMI demographics by heating type"
reference-location: margin
fig-cap-location: bottom
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This side notebook profiles the **low-and-moderate-income (LMI)** subpopulation of the NY ResStock sample by primary heating fuel. It answers two questions advocates have asked for the rate-design filing:

1. How are LMI households distributed across heating fuels — heat pumps, electric resistance, natural gas, oil/propane?
2. Within each heating-fuel cohort, what share of LMI households **rent** their home?

::: {.callout-note}
LMI assignment in this analysis is derived from ResStock's inferred household income, federal-poverty-level, occupants, and tenure characteristics — it was not the focus of the heat-pump rates analysis and required several modeling assumptions. Treat the percentages here as approximate. Renter share comes from ResStock tenure metadata; rows with missing tenure are reported as "unknown tenure" rather than imputed.
:::

## Parameters

```{python}
#| label: params

STATE = "NY"
YEAR = 2025

### UPDATE WITH NEW RUNS
BATCH = "ny_20260416a_r1-36"
RDP_REF = "205041d"  # rate-design-platform git ref for fetch_rdp_file
###

_state_lower = STATE.lower()
S3_BASE = "s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates"
_BILLS = f"{S3_BASE}/{_state_lower}/all_utilities/{BATCH}"

PATH_MASTER_BILLS_BASELINE = f"{_BILLS}/run_1+2/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_METADATA_SB = (
    "s3://data.sb/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2_sb/"
    "metadata_utility/state=NY/utility_assignment.parquet"
)

md_sb_cols = ["bldg_id", "in.tenure", "postprocess_group.heating_type"]

HEATING_TYPE_COL = "postprocess_group.heating_type"
```

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup

from __future__ import annotations

import polars as pl
from great_tables import GT

from lib.great_tables import get_switchbox_gt_tab_options
from lib.quarto import display_gt
```

## Load LMI households from master bills

We pull annual rows for the baseline scenario (`upgrade == 0`, `month == "Annual"`) from the run 1+2 master bills, filter to LMI households (`is_lmi_any`), and keep just the columns needed to assign each home a heating bucket.

```{python}
#| label: load-bills

bills_lmi = (
    pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_BASELINE, hive_partitioning=True)
    .filter(
        (pl.col("month") == "Annual")
        & (pl.col("upgrade") == 0)
        & (pl.col("is_lmi_any"))
    )
    .select(
        "bldg_id",
        pl.col("weight").alias("resstock_weight"),
        HEATING_TYPE_COL,
        "heats_with_natgas",
        "heats_with_oil",
        "heats_with_propane",
    )
    .collect()
)

print(f"LMI households (raw rows): {bills_lmi.height:,}")
print(f"Total ResStock weight: {bills_lmi['resstock_weight'].sum():,.0f}")
bills_lmi.head(5)
```

## Load tenure metadata

`in.tenure` is not preserved in the master bills, so we pull it from the per-state utility assignment metadata.

```{python}
#| label: load-meta

meta_sb = pl.read_parquet(PATH_METADATA_SB, columns=md_sb_cols)
print(f"meta_sb: {meta_sb.height:,} rows")
meta_sb["in.tenure"].value_counts().sort("count", descending=True)
```

## Join, bucket, and flag tenure

We join the LMI household rows on `bldg_id`, derive a mutually-exclusive `heating_bucket`, and flag renters and unknown-tenure rows. Buckets follow the convention used elsewhere in the analysis (see `analysis.qmd`): heat pumps and electric resistance are read directly off `postprocess_group.heating_type`; the fossil-fuel bucket is split by which fuel flag is set.

```{python}
#| label: join-bucket

HEATING_BUCKET_ORDER = [
    "Heat pump",
    "Electric resistance",
    "Natural gas",
    "Oil / propane",
    "Other",
]

heating_bucket_expr = (
    pl.when(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "heat_pump")
    .then(pl.lit("Heat pump"))
    .when(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == "electrical_resistance")
    .then(pl.lit("Electric resistance"))
    .when(pl.col("heats_with_natgas"))
    .then(pl.lit("Natural gas"))
    .when(pl.col("heats_with_oil") | pl.col("heats_with_propane"))
    .then(pl.lit("Oil / propane"))
    .otherwise(pl.lit("Other"))
    .alias("heating_bucket")
)

df_lmi = (
    bills_lmi
    .join(meta_sb.select("bldg_id", "in.tenure"), on="bldg_id", how="left")
    .with_columns(
        heating_bucket_expr,
        (pl.col("in.tenure") == "Renter").alias("is_renter"),
        ((pl.col("in.tenure") == "Not Available") | pl.col("in.tenure").is_null()).alias("is_tenure_unknown"),
    )
)

df_lmi.head(5)
```

## Aggregate

We compute weighted counts and tenure shares per `heating_bucket`, then append an "All LMI" total row. Tenure denominators are all LMI households in each bucket, so `% renter + % owner + % unknown = 100%`.

```{python}
#| label: aggregate

def _bucket_aggregates(df: pl.DataFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Weighted LMI counts and tenure shares per heating_bucket."""
    return df.group_by("heating_bucket").agg(
        pl.col("resstock_weight").sum().alias("n_lmi_households"),
        ((pl.col("is_renter").cast(pl.Float64) * pl.col("resstock_weight")).sum()
         / pl.col("resstock_weight").sum()).alias("pct_renter"),
        ((pl.col("is_tenure_unknown").cast(pl.Float64) * pl.col("resstock_weight")).sum()
         / pl.col("resstock_weight").sum()).alias("pct_unknown_tenure"),
    )


by_bucket = _bucket_aggregates(df_lmi)
total_lmi_count = float(df_lmi["resstock_weight"].sum())

all_lmi_row = pl.DataFrame({
    "heating_bucket": ["All LMI"],
    "n_lmi_households": [total_lmi_count],
    "pct_renter": [
        float((df_lmi["is_renter"].cast(pl.Float64) * df_lmi["resstock_weight"]).sum())
        / total_lmi_count
    ],
    "pct_unknown_tenure": [
        float((df_lmi["is_tenure_unknown"].cast(pl.Float64) * df_lmi["resstock_weight"]).sum())
        / total_lmi_count
    ],
})

bucket_dtype = pl.Enum([*HEATING_BUCKET_ORDER, "All LMI"])

agg = (
    pl.concat([by_bucket, all_lmi_row], how="vertical")
    .with_columns(
        (pl.col("n_lmi_households") / total_lmi_count).alias("share_of_lmi"),
        (1 - pl.col("pct_renter") - pl.col("pct_unknown_tenure")).alias("pct_owner"),
        pl.col("heating_bucket").cast(bucket_dtype),
    )
    .sort("heating_bucket")
    .select(
        "heating_bucket",
        "n_lmi_households",
        "share_of_lmi",
        "pct_renter",
        "pct_owner",
        "pct_unknown_tenure",
    )
)

show_unknown_tenure = bool((agg["pct_unknown_tenure"] > 0).any())

agg
```

## Combined summary

Heating-fuel mix and tenure breakdown of NY LMI households in one view.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-lmi-by-heating
#| tbl-cap: "Heating-fuel mix and tenure of NY LMI households. Heating type and tenure come from NREL's ResStock metadata @nrel_EndUseLoadProfiles_2024; LMI status was inferred from household income relative to the Federal Poverty Line."

combined_cols = [
    "heating_bucket",
    "n_lmi_households",
    "share_of_lmi",
    "pct_renter",
    "pct_owner",
]
combined_pct_cols = ["share_of_lmi", "pct_renter", "pct_owner"]
combined_labels = {
    "n_lmi_households": "LMI households (weighted)",
    "share_of_lmi": "Share of LMI",
    "pct_renter": "% renter",
    "pct_owner": "% owner",
}
if show_unknown_tenure:
    combined_cols.append("pct_unknown_tenure")
    combined_pct_cols.append("pct_unknown_tenure")
    combined_labels["pct_unknown_tenure"] = "% unknown tenure"

_combined_gt = (
    GT(agg.select(combined_cols), rowname_col="heating_bucket")
    .fmt_number(columns="n_lmi_households", decimals=0, use_seps=True)
    .fmt_percent(
        columns=combined_pct_cols,
        decimals=0,
    )
    .cols_label(**combined_labels)
    .tab_stubhead(label="Heating fuel")
    .tab_source_note(
        "Data source: National Laboratory of the Rockies's ResStock 2024 AMY2018 release 2; LMI status was inferred from household income and vulnerability indicators."
    )
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
display_gt(_combined_gt)
```

## Heating-fuel distribution within LMI

What heats LMI homes in NY?

```{python}
#| label: tbl-lmi-heating-share
#| tbl-cap: "Heating-fuel mix of NY LMI households. Heating type comes from NREL's ResStock metadata @nrel_EndUseLoadProfiles_2024; LMI status was inferred from household income relative to the Federal Poverty Line."

_heating_share_gt = (
    GT(
        agg.select("heating_bucket", "n_lmi_households", "share_of_lmi"),
        rowname_col="heating_bucket",
    )
    .fmt_number(columns="n_lmi_households", decimals=0, use_seps=True)
    .fmt_percent(columns="share_of_lmi", decimals=0)
    .cols_label(
        n_lmi_households="LMI households (weighted)",
        share_of_lmi="Share of LMI",
    )
    .tab_stubhead(label="Heating fuel")
    .tab_source_note("Data source: National Laboratory of the Rockies's ResStock 2024 AMY2018 release 2; LMI status was inferred from household income and vulnerability indicators.")
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
display_gt(_heating_share_gt)
```

## Tenure breakdown within LMI

What share of LMI households rent or own? `% unknown tenure` is shown if any LMI households have missing tenure.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-lmi-tenure
#| tbl-cap: "Tenure of NY LMI households. Tenure comes from NREL's ResStock metadata @nrel_EndUseLoadProfiles_2024; LMI status was inferred from household income relative to the Federal Poverty Line."

tenure_rows = [
    {"tenure": "Renter", "n_lmi_households": total_lmi_count * float(all_lmi_row["pct_renter"][0])},
    {"tenure": "Owner", "n_lmi_households": total_lmi_count * (1 - float(all_lmi_row["pct_renter"][0]) - float(all_lmi_row["pct_unknown_tenure"][0]))},
]
if show_unknown_tenure:
    tenure_rows.append({
        "tenure": "Unknown tenure",
        "n_lmi_households": total_lmi_count * float(all_lmi_row["pct_unknown_tenure"][0]),
    })

tenure_summary = (
    pl.DataFrame(tenure_rows)
    .with_columns((pl.col("n_lmi_households") / total_lmi_count).alias("share_of_lmi"))
)

_tenure_gt = (
    GT(tenure_summary, rowname_col="tenure")
    .fmt_number(columns="n_lmi_households", decimals=0, use_seps=True)
    .fmt_percent(columns="share_of_lmi", decimals=0)
    .cols_label(
        n_lmi_households="LMI households (weighted)",
        share_of_lmi="Share of LMI",
    )
    .tab_stubhead(label="Tenure")
    .tab_source_note("Data source: National Laboratory of the Rockies's ResStock 2024 AMY2018 release 2; LMI status was inferred from household income and vulnerability indicators.")
    .tab_options(**get_switchbox_gt_tab_options())
)
display_gt(_tenure_gt)
```
